blended.active_ads.label: Active Ads
blended.active_ads.formula: Active Ads = count of distinct ad IDs that served ≥1 impression in the period (Google + Meta combined)

blended.active_audiences.label: Active Audiences
blended.active_audiences.formula: Active Audiences = count of distinct audience IDs with activity in the period (Google ad groups + Meta ad sets)

blended.active_campaigns.label: Active Campaigns
blended.active_campaigns.formula: Active Campaigns = count of distinct campaigns with any activity (spend, revenue, impressions, or clicks) in the period, across Google + Meta

blended.audience_conversions.label: Audience Conversions
blended.audience_conversions.formula: Audience Conversions = sum of conversions credited to audience-targeted campaigns over the period (Google + Meta)

blended.budget_at_risk.label: Budget at Risk
blended.budget_at_risk.formula: Budget at Risk = total spend on campaigns in the Optimize/Cut quadrant (high spend, low ROAS); % of Total = budget at risk ÷ total spend × 100

blended.cac.label: Blended CAC
blended.cac.formula: Blended CAC = total ad spend (Google + Meta) ÷ new customers acquired

blended.cac_efficiency.label: CAC Efficiency
blended.cac_efficiency.formula: CAC Efficiency = first-purchase AOV ÷ blended CAC (ratio above 1 means the first order alone recoups acquisition cost)

blended.conversion_rate.label: Site Conversion Rate
blended.conversion_rate.formula: Site Conversion Rate = orders ÷ website sessions × 100; Change % vs comparison period

blended.conversions.label: Total Conversions
blended.conversions.formula: Total Conversions = sum of platform-reported conversions across Google + Meta

blended.cpa.label: Average CPA
blended.cpa.formula: Average CPA = (Google spend + Meta spend) ÷ total platform-reported conversions

blended.ctr.label: Blended CTR
blended.ctr.formula: Blended CTR = total clicks ÷ total impressions × 100 (impression-weighted across Google + Meta)

blended.ga4_magento_gap.label: GA4 vs Magento Gap
blended.ga4_magento_gap.formula: GA4 vs Magento Gap = total GA4 revenue − Magento store revenue; Gap % = gap ÷ Magento revenue × 100

blended.google_attribution_gap.label: Google Attribution Gap
blended.google_attribution_gap.formula: Google Attribution Gap = Google platform-reported revenue − GA4 Google revenue; Gap % = gap ÷ GA4 Google revenue × 100

blended.klaviyo_attribution_gap.label: Klaviyo Attribution Gap
blended.klaviyo_attribution_gap.formula: Klaviyo Attribution Gap = Klaviyo self-reported revenue − GA4 email revenue; Gap % = gap ÷ GA4 email revenue × 100

blended.mer.label: Marketing Efficiency Ratio
blended.mer.formula: Marketing Efficiency Ratio = total Magento store revenue ÷ (Google spend + Meta spend)

blended.meta_attribution_gap.label: Meta Attribution Gap
blended.meta_attribution_gap.formula: Meta Attribution Gap = Meta platform-reported revenue − GA4 Meta revenue; Gap % = gap ÷ GA4 Meta revenue × 100

blended.optimize_cut_campaigns.label: Optimize/Cut Campaigns
blended.optimize_cut_campaigns.formula: Optimize/Cut Campaigns = count of campaigns with spend ≥ median spend and ROAS < median ROAS (medians taken over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS)

blended.purchase_reconciliation.label: Purchase Reconciliation
blended.purchase_reconciliation.formula: Purchase Reconciliation Delta = GA4 purchases − Magento orders; Gap % = delta ÷ Magento orders × 100

blended.revenue_reconciliation.label: Revenue Reconciliation
blended.revenue_reconciliation.formula: Revenue Reconciliation headline = total Magento store revenue (source of truth), with gap percentages versus platform-reported and GA4-attributed revenue

blended.roas.label: Blended ROAS
blended.roas.formula: Blended ROAS = weekly ad revenue ÷ weekly ad spend (Google + Meta); WoW Change %

blended.scale_campaigns.label: Scale Campaigns
blended.scale_campaigns.formula: Scale Campaigns = count of campaigns with spend ≥ median spend and ROAS ≥ median ROAS (medians taken over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS)

blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.label: Scale Opportunity Campaigns
blended.scale_opportunity_campaigns.formula: Scale Opportunity Campaigns = count of campaigns with spend < median spend and ROAS ≥ median ROAS (also includes campaigns with zero spend but positive revenue); medians over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS

blended.test_kill_campaigns.label: Test/Kill Campaigns
blended.test_kill_campaigns.formula: Test/Kill Campaigns = count of campaigns with spend < median spend and ROAS < median ROAS (medians taken over campaigns with positive spend and ROAS)

blended.total_ad_spend.label: Total Ad Spend
blended.total_ad_spend.formula: Total Ad Spend = combined Google Ads + Meta Ads spend for the latest week; WoW Change %

blended.yoy_revenue_change.label: YoY Change
blended.yoy_revenue_change.formula: YoY Change % = (most recent completed year revenue − prior year revenue) ÷ prior year revenue × 100

derived.estimated_orders.label: Weekly Orders
derived.estimated_orders.formula: Weekly Orders (estimated) = weekly revenue ÷ $50 assumed average order value; WoW Change % = revenue WoW

derived.gross_margin.label: Gross Margin
derived.gross_margin.formula: Gross Margin % (estimated) = (net revenue × 50% assumed COGS − total ad spend) ÷ net revenue × 100

derived.ltv_cac_ratio.label: LTV:CAC Ratio
derived.ltv_cac_ratio.formula: LTV:CAC = estimated LTV ÷ CAC, where LTV = (net revenue ÷ orders) × 1.5 and CAC = total ad spend ÷ new users

derived.projected_revenue.label: Projected Revenue
derived.projected_revenue.formula: Projected Revenue = (Google budget × Google ROAS) + (Meta budget × Meta ROAS) + (Email budget × Email ROAS), where budget split is 52% Google / 46% Meta / 2% Email and channel ROAS are prior-year actuals (Email fixed at 25x)

derived.revenue_per_session.label: Revenue per Session
derived.revenue_per_session.formula: Revenue per Session = revenue ÷ sessions over the period

ga4.avg_price.label: Average Item Price
ga4.avg_price.formula: Average Item Price = category revenue ÷ items sold (level-3 categories, over the period)

ga4.avg_revenue_per_new_product.label: Avg Revenue Per Product
ga4.avg_revenue_per_new_product.formula: Avg Revenue Per Product = total revenue from new products ÷ count of distinct new products (each new product's activity from its launch date through the end date)

ga4.bounce_rate.label: Bounce Rate
ga4.bounce_rate.formula: Bounce Rate = (1 − engaged sessions ÷ sessions) × 100 (weighted across the period)

ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate.label: Cart-to-Purchase Rate
ga4.cart_to_purchase_rate.formula: Cart-to-Purchase Rate = average across qualifying products of (each product's units purchased ÷ its cart adds) × 100, over the last 30 days, limited to items with 50–10,000 cart adds and at least one purchase

ga4.checkout_completion_rate.label: Checkout Completion Rate
ga4.checkout_completion_rate.formula: Checkout Completion Rate = purchases ÷ begun checkouts × 100

ga4.conversion_rate.label: Conversion Rate
ga4.conversion_rate.formula: Conversion Rate = GA4 purchases ÷ GA4 sessions × 100

ga4.declining_items.label: Declining Items
ga4.declining_items.formula: Declining Items = count of distinct products whose current-week units purchased fell below half of their level four weeks earlier, among items that had more than 20 purchases four weeks ago (fixed trailing 60-day window anchored to today, independent of the dashboard date filter)

ga4.direct_share.label: Direct Traffic Share
ga4.direct_share.formula: Direct Traffic Share = GA4 Direct revenue ÷ total GA4 revenue × 100

ga4.engagement_rate.label: Engagement Rate
ga4.engagement_rate.formula: Engagement Rate = (engaged sessions ÷ sessions) × 100 (weighted across the period)

ga4.estimated_lost_revenue.label: Est. Lost Revenue
ga4.estimated_lost_revenue.formula: Estimated Lost Revenue = sum over items with >50 cart adds of (benchmark conversion − item's own conversion) × the item's cart adds × the item's price, over the last 30 days, where benchmark conversion = average of units purchased ÷ cart adds across all items with >50 adds, and price = item revenue ÷ units purchased (falling back to the catalog average price when the item has no purchases)

ga4.funnel_drop_rate.label: Funnel Drop Rate
ga4.funnel_drop_rate.formula: Funnel Drop Rate = (1 − purchase events ÷ add-to-cart events) × 100, sitewide over the last 30 days

ga4.items_at_risk.label: Items At Risk
ga4.items_at_risk.formula: Items At Risk = count of distinct products with zero units purchased but more than 50 cart adds (cart adds capped below 10,000 to exclude anomalies) over the last 30 days

ga4.new_products_count.label: New Products Launched
ga4.new_products_count.formula: New Products Launched = count of distinct products whose first-ever appearance falls within the launch window (placeholder IDs '(not set)' and '(other)' excluded; a product launches on its earliest activity date)

ga4.new_products_revenue.label: Total Revenue
ga4.new_products_revenue.formula: Total Revenue = sum of revenue across all new products from each product's launch date through the end date (new product = first appearance within the launch window, placeholders excluded)

ga4.new_products_success_rate.label: Success Rate
ga4.new_products_success_rate.formula: Success Rate = (count of new products that earned any revenue since launch ÷ total new products in the window) × 100

ga4.product_conversion_rate.label: Purchase Rate
ga4.product_conversion_rate.formula: Purchase Rate = (items purchased ÷ items viewed) × 100 (level-3 categories)

ga4.revenue.label: Weekly Revenue
ga4.revenue.formula: Weekly Revenue = GA4 channel-attributed revenue across all channels for the week; WoW Change %

ga4.sessions.label: Weekly Sessions
ga4.sessions.formula: Weekly Sessions = total website sessions from all traffic sources for the week; WoW Change %

ga4.true_oos_count.label: True OOS Count
ga4.true_oos_count.formula: True OOS Count = count of distinct products with zero units purchased but more than 100 cart adds (cart adds capped below 10,000) over the last 30 days — a stricter bar than Items At Risk

ga4.units_sold.label: Items Sold
ga4.units_sold.formula: Items Sold = sum of items purchased across level-3 categories over the period

ga4.users.label: Users
ga4.users.formula: Users = sum of unique users over the period

google_ads.absolute_top_impression_rate.label: Google Ads — absolute top impression rate

google_ads.active_campaigns.label: Campaigns with Spend
google_ads.active_campaigns.formula: Campaigns with Spend = count of distinct Shopping campaigns with spend > 0 over the period (compared against total Shopping campaigns)

google_ads.below_top_impression_rate.label: Below Top Position
google_ads.below_top_impression_rate.formula: Below Top Position % = 100 − top-position rate, where top-position rate = impression-weighted average of the share of impressions landing above organic results

google_ads.clicks.label: Clicks
google_ads.clicks.formula: Clicks = total Google Ads clicks over the selected period

google_ads.conversion_value.label: Shopping Revenue
google_ads.conversion_value.formula: Shopping Revenue = total conversion value from Shopping campaigns over the selected period

google_ads.conversions.label: Conversions
google_ads.conversions.formula: Conversions = total conversions attributed to Google Ads campaigns over the selected period

google_ads.cpa.label: CPA
google_ads.cpa.formula: CPA = total Google Ads spend ÷ total conversions

google_ads.cpv.label: Cost Per View
google_ads.cpv.formula: Cost Per View = total YouTube spend ÷ total video views

google_ads.impressions.label: Impressions
google_ads.impressions.formula: Impressions = total ad impressions across all campaigns over the period (Google Search)

google_ads.position_gap_rate.label: Position Gap
google_ads.position_gap_rate.formula: Position Gap % = top-position rate − absolute-top (#1) rate (both impression-weighted)

google_ads.quality_score.label: Quality Score
google_ads.quality_score.formula: Quality Score = average Quality Score (1-10) across scored Search keywords

google_ads.roas.label: ROAS
google_ads.roas.formula: ROAS = total conversion value ÷ total Google Ads spend

google_ads.spend.label: Spend
google_ads.spend.formula: Spend = total Google Ads spend across all campaigns over the selected period

google_ads.video_views.label: Video Views
google_ads.video_views.formula: Video Views = total video views across YouTube/Video campaigns over the selected period

google_ads.view_rate.label: View Rate
google_ads.view_rate.formula: View Rate = total video views ÷ total impressions × 100

klaviyo.active_flows.label: Active Flows
klaviyo.active_flows.formula: Active Flows = count of distinct automation flows in live status that sent during the period

klaviyo.bounce_rate.label: Bounce Rate
klaviyo.bounce_rate.formula: Bounce Rate = bounced emails ÷ emails sent × 100

klaviyo.click_rate.label: Click Rate
klaviyo.click_rate.formula: Click Rate = unique clicks ÷ emails sent × 100

klaviyo.ctor.label: Click-to-Open Rate (CTOR)
klaviyo.ctor.formula: Click-to-Open Rate = unique clicks ÷ unique opens × 100

klaviyo.open_rate.label: Open Rate
klaviyo.open_rate.formula: Open Rate = unique opens ÷ emails sent × 100

klaviyo.revenue_per_sms.label: Revenue per SMS
klaviyo.revenue_per_sms.formula: Revenue per SMS = SMS-attributed revenue ÷ total SMS sent

klaviyo.sends.label: Emails Sent
klaviyo.sends.formula: Emails Sent = total emails sent over the period

klaviyo.sms_revenue.label: SMS Revenue
klaviyo.sms_revenue.formula: SMS Revenue = total order value from Placed Order events attributed to recipients who received an SMS beforehand

klaviyo.sms_sent.label: SMS Sent
klaviyo.sms_sent.formula: SMS Sent = count of distinct received SMS messages over the period

klaviyo.unsubscribe_rate.label: Unsubscribe Rate
klaviyo.unsubscribe_rate.formula: SMS Unsubscribe Rate = distinct SMS opt-out events ÷ distinct received SMS × 100

magento.active_customers.label: Active Customers
magento.active_customers.formula: Active Customers = count of distinct customers (with ≥1 order) whose most recent order is within 90 days of the period end. Active Rate = active customers ÷ total customers × 100

magento.aov.label: Average Order Value
magento.aov.formula: Average Order Value = total Magento revenue ÷ number of Magento orders

magento.at_risk_clv.label: At-Risk Value
magento.at_risk_clv.formula: At-Risk Value = sum of net lifetime value of customers flagged High or Critical churn risk, scoped to the acquisition cohort in the selected period

magento.average_ltv.label: Average LTV
magento.average_ltv.formula: Average LTV = gross revenue ÷ unique customers

magento.avg_customer_lifespan_days.label: Avg Days Between Orders
magento.avg_customer_lifespan_days.formula: Avg Days Between Orders = average customer lifespan in days (first-to-last order gap), averaged across customers with ≥1 order

magento.avg_days_between_orders.label: Avg Days Between Orders
magento.avg_days_between_orders.formula: Avg Days Between Orders = average of the daily per-segment average gap between a customer's orders, over rows that have a positive value

magento.avg_items_per_order.label: Avg Items per Order
magento.avg_items_per_order.formula: Avg Items per Order = total items ordered ÷ total orders (over the period)

magento.avg_orders_per_customer.label: Avg Orders per Customer
magento.avg_orders_per_customer.formula: Avg Orders per Customer = average number of orders across customers with ≥1 order

magento.avg_rating.label: Average Rating
magento.avg_rating.formula: Average Rating = average of each day's average star rating (1-5) over the trailing 7 days; change = current 7-day average − prior 7-day average

magento.bundle_pct.label: Bundle Penetration
magento.bundle_pct.formula: Bundle Penetration = (orders containing a bundle product ÷ total orders) × 100

magento.cancellation_rate.label: Cancellation Rate
magento.cancellation_rate.formula: Cancellation Rate = canceled orders ÷ total orders × 100

magento.cart_abandonment_rate.label: Cart Abandonment Rate
magento.cart_abandonment_rate.formula: Cart Abandonment Rate = abandoned carts ÷ (abandoned carts + converted carts) × 100

magento.churn_risk_score.label: Average Churn Risk
magento.churn_risk_score.formula: Average Churn Risk = average of the per-customer churn-risk score (0-100) across purchasing customers active in the period

magento.clv.label: Average CLV
magento.clv.formula: Average CLV = average net lifetime value across customers with positive lifetime value

magento.clv_at_risk.label: CLV at Risk
magento.clv_at_risk.formula: CLV at Risk = sum of net lifetime value of customers at Critical churn-risk level (churn-risk score ≥ 80)

magento.completed_orders.label: Completed Orders
magento.completed_orders.formula: Completed Orders = count of orders that reached the completed status over the period

magento.conversion_rate.label: Conversion Rate
magento.conversion_rate.formula: Conversion Rate = converting touchpoints ÷ all journey touchpoints × 100

magento.critical_risk_count.label: Critical Risk Customers
magento.critical_risk_count.formula: Critical Risk Customers = count of customers at Critical churn-risk level (churn-risk score ≥ 80)

magento.customers.label: Total Customers
magento.customers.formula: Total Customers = count of distinct customers with ≥1 order who had any order activity overlapping the selected period

magento.customers_with_clv.label: Customers with CLV
magento.customers_with_clv.formula: Customers with CLV = count of acquisition-cohort customers that have a non-null lifetime value

magento.customers_with_scores.label: Customers with Scores
magento.customers_with_scores.formula: Customers with Scores = count of customers that have a computed health score

magento.engagement_rate.label: Engagement Rate
magento.engagement_rate.formula: Engagement Rate = touchpoints with a non-zero value ÷ all journey touchpoints × 100 (proxy for engagement, not a native field)

magento.first_purchase_aov.label: First Purchase AOV
magento.first_purchase_aov.formula: First Purchase AOV = total first-order revenue ÷ new customers acquired

magento.gross_revenue.label: Total Revenue
magento.gross_revenue.formula: Total Revenue = sum of gross order revenue over the period

magento.guest_checkout_rate.label: Guest Checkout Rate
magento.guest_checkout_rate.formula: Guest Checkout Rate = orders placed by guest customers ÷ total orders × 100

magento.health_score.label: Average Health Score
magento.health_score.formula: Average Health Score = average of the per-customer health score (0-100) across scored customers

magento.high_risk_customers.label: High-Risk Customers
magento.high_risk_customers.formula: High-Risk Customers = count of customers at High or Critical churn-risk level (churn-risk score ≥ 60); percentage = high-risk count ÷ customers with a prediction × 100

magento.net_revenue.label: Total Revenue
magento.net_revenue.formula: Total Revenue = sum of Magento net revenue over the period

magento.new_customer_order_share.label: New Customer Revenue %
magento.new_customer_order_share.formula: New Customer Revenue % = new-customer orders ÷ total orders × 100 (order-share proxy)

magento.new_customers.label: New Customers
magento.new_customers.formula: New Customers = count of distinct customers whose first order falls within the selected period

magento.nps_score.label: NPS Score
magento.nps_score.formula: NPS Score = average product-review star rating (1-5) over the period, used as a satisfaction proxy (not a true -100..+100 NPS); change = current average − prior average

magento.orders.label: Total Orders
magento.orders.formula: Total Orders = count of all orders placed over the period

magento.orders_per_customer.label: Avg Orders per Customer
magento.orders_per_customer.formula: Avg Orders per Customer = total orders ÷ unique customers

magento.predicted_clv.label: Predicted CLV
magento.predicted_clv.formula: Predicted CLV = sum of realized (actual) lifetime value across the acquisition cohort — placeholder; no forward-looking prediction exists in the mart, so it equals Total CLV

magento.processing_orders.label: Processing Orders
magento.processing_orders.formula: Processing Orders = max(0, total orders − completed orders) — orders received but not yet completed (no separate canceled-order count is available, so canceled is treated as 0)

magento.promoter_percent.label: Promoters
magento.promoter_percent.formula: Promoters % = reviews rated 4 or 5 stars ÷ all rated reviews × 100%; change = current % − prior %

magento.refund_rate.label: Refund Rate
magento.refund_rate.formula: Refund Rate = refunded orders ÷ total orders × 100

magento.repeat_rate.label: Repeat Rate
magento.repeat_rate.formula: Repeat Rate = repeat customers ÷ unique customers × 100

magento.returning_customers.label: Returning Customers
magento.returning_customers.formula: Returning Customers = count of distinct customers acquired before the period who placed an order within the period. Retention Rate = returning customers ÷ total customers × 100

magento.sessions.label: Total Sessions
magento.sessions.formula: Total Sessions = count of journey touchpoint rows in the period (each order touchpoint counts as one session)

magento.top_affinity_lift.label: Top Affinity Lift
magento.top_affinity_lift.formula: Top Affinity Lift = highest lift score among product pairs with at least 5 co-purchases, where lift = probability of buying A and B together ÷ (probability of A × probability of B)

magento.total_clv.label: Total CLV
magento.total_clv.formula: Total CLV = sum of realized (actual) lifetime value across all acquisition-cohort customers in the selected period

magento.total_responses.label: Survey Responses
magento.total_responses.formula: Survey Responses = count of rated reviews (rating above zero) submitted in the period; change % = (current − previous) ÷ previous × 100

magento.units_sold.label: Units Sold
magento.units_sold.formula: Units Sold = total product units sold over the period

magento.users.label: Total Users
magento.users.formula: Total Users = count of distinct customers appearing in the journey data during the period

meta_ads.active_creatives.label: Active Creatives
meta_ads.active_creatives.formula: Active Creatives = count of distinct creatives with ad spend > 0 in the trailing 7 days

meta_ads.cost_per_1k_reach.label: Cost per 1k Reach
meta_ads.cost_per_1k_reach.formula: Cost per 1k Reach = Meta prospecting spend ÷ reach × 1,000 (cost to reach 1,000 unique people)

meta_ads.cpc.label: Avg CPC
meta_ads.cpc.formula: Avg CPC = Meta creative spend ÷ link clicks (cost per link click)

meta_ads.ctr.label: Retargeting CTR
meta_ads.ctr.formula: Retargeting CTR = average of daily ad-set click-through rate across retargeting campaigns (percentage)

meta_ads.frequency.label: Average Frequency
meta_ads.frequency.formula: Average Frequency = Meta impressions ÷ Meta reach (Meta only)

meta_ads.reach.label: Total Reach
meta_ads.reach.formula: Total Reach = sum of daily Meta reach over the period (Meta only; approximate, daily reach can overlap across days)

meta_ads.retargeting_fatigue.label: Retargeting Fatigue
meta_ads.retargeting_fatigue.formula: Retargeting Fatigue Score = (historical-period CTR − current-period CTR) ÷ historical-period CTR × 100

meta_ads.roas.label: ROAS
meta_ads.roas.formula: ROAS = Meta attributed conversion value ÷ Meta spend (7-day click + 1-day view attribution)

meta_ads.spend.label: Total Spend
meta_ads.spend.formula: Total Spend = total Meta advertising spend across all placements over the period

meta_ads.total_creatives.label: Total Creatives
meta_ads.total_creatives.formula: Total Creatives = count of distinct creative assets that ran in ads over the period

ml.acquisition_forecast.label: Forecast New Customers (next 4w)
ml.acquisition_forecast.formula: Forecast New Customers (next 4w) = sum of the predicted new customers across every entry category over the next four weeks

ml.acquisition_forecast_avg_per_category.label: Avg / Entry Category
ml.acquisition_forecast_avg_per_category.formula: Avg per Entry Category = total forecast new customers ÷ number of forecast entry categories

ml.acquisition_forecast_top10_concentrat.label: Top-10 Concentration
ml.acquisition_forecast_top10_concentrat.formula: Top-10 Concentration = forecast new customers of the 10 highest entry categories ÷ total forecast new customers × 100

ml.acquisition_forecast_top_category.label: Top Entry Category
ml.acquisition_forecast_top_category.formula: Top Entry Category = the entry category with the highest forecast new customers, with its share = that category's forecast ÷ total forecast new customers

ml.actionable_customers.label: Actionable Customers
ml.actionable_customers.formula: Actionable Customers = count of customers whose predicted intervention propensity is at or above the optimal threshold (≈11.4%)

ml.activation_propensity.label: Avg Activation Propensity
ml.activation_propensity.formula: Avg Activation Propensity = average predicted first-purchase (activation) propensity across all scored prospects

ml.active_customers.label: Active Customers
ml.active_customers.formula: Active Customers = count of customers whose predicted lifecycle stage is 'Active'

ml.aov_uplift_vs_historical_pct.label: Predicted Growth vs Past
ml.aov_uplift_vs_historical_pct.formula: Predicted Growth vs Past = (predicted average order value − historical average order value) ÷ historical average order value × 100; blank when no historical baseline exists

ml.ascending_trajectory_customers.label: Ascending Customers
ml.ascending_trajectory_customers.formula: Ascending Customers = count of customers predicted to move to a higher value trajectory (predicted class upgrade or big_upgrade)

ml.at_risk_customers.label: At-Risk Customers
ml.at_risk_customers.formula: At-Risk Customers = count of customers the lifecycle model flags as at risk of churning in the prediction horizon

ml.attributed_revenue.label: Paid Revenue
ml.attributed_revenue.formula: Paid Revenue = sum of model-attributed revenue across all paid channels (Google, Meta, Email)

ml.avg_days_until_risk.label: Avg Days Until Risk
ml.avg_days_until_risk.formula: Avg Days Until Risk = average projected days-until-stock-out across the Critical/High-risk products

ml.avg_grow_probability.label: Avg Growth Probability
ml.avg_grow_probability.formula: Avg Growth Probability = average model-predicted probability of growing wallet share across all scored customers, shown as a percent

ml.avg_optimal_markdown_depth.label: Avg Optimal Markdown Depth
ml.avg_optimal_markdown_depth.formula: Avg Optimal Markdown Depth = average of the model's recommended markdown % across all products

ml.avg_predicted_aov.label: Expected Avg Order
ml.avg_predicted_aov.formula: Expected Avg Order = mean predicted next order value across all scored customers

ml.avg_seasonality_index.label: Avg Seasonality Index
ml.avg_seasonality_index.formula: Avg Seasonality Index = average of every category's 12 monthly seasonality index values (index 1.0 = a month at that category's yearly average)

ml.avg_stage_duration_days.label: Avg Stage Duration
ml.avg_stage_duration_days.formula: Avg Stage Duration = average number of days customers remain in their current lifecycle stage

ml.best_day_of_week.label: Best Day of Week
ml.best_day_of_week.formula: Best Day of Week = the most common predicted best-response weekday across customers (mode of each customer's best day)

ml.brand_affinity_multi_brand_customers.label: Multi-Brand Customers
ml.brand_affinity_multi_brand_customers.formula: Multi-Brand Customers = count of scored customers the model classifies as Explorers or Switchers (buying across multiple brands)

ml.brand_affinity_single_brand_loyalists.label: Single Brand Loyalists
ml.brand_affinity_single_brand_loyalists.formula: Single Brand Loyalists = count of scored customers the model classifies as Loyalists (loyalty type = Loyalist)

ml.brand_affinity_switcher_pool.label: Brand Switcher Pool
ml.brand_affinity_switcher_pool.formula: Brand Switcher Pool = count of scored customers the model classifies as brand Switchers (loyalty type = Switcher)

ml.brand_affinity_top_brand.label: Top Brand by Affinity
ml.brand_affinity_top_brand.formula: Top Brand by Affinity = the single brand most frequently identified as a customer's top brand across all scored customers (most common top brand)

ml.break_even_discount.label: Break-Even Discount
ml.break_even_discount.formula: Break-Even Discount = the shallowest discount depth where net revenue first turns non-negative (uplift just covers the discount cost)

ml.buyer_breadth_categories_forecast.label: Categories Forecast
ml.buyer_breadth_categories_forecast.formula: Categories Forecast = count of distinct categories that have a next-4-week buyer forecast

ml.buyer_breadth_forecast_avg_per_catego.label: Avg / Category
ml.buyer_breadth_forecast_avg_per_catego.formula: Avg per Category = total forecast buyers ÷ number of forecast categories

ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.label: Top-10 Concentration
ml.buyer_breadth_top10_concentration.formula: Top-10 Concentration = forecast buyers of the 10 highest categories ÷ total forecast buyers

ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.label: Top Category
ml.buyer_breadth_top_category.formula: Top Category = the category with the highest forecast buyers, with its share = that category's forecast buyers ÷ total forecast buyers

ml.cart_recovery_high_medium_pool.label: High+Medium Tier Pool
ml.cart_recovery_high_medium_pool.formula: High+Medium Tier Pool = count of abandoned carts in the high and medium recovery-score tiers combined (the pool most worth a recovery touch)

ml.cart_recovery_score.label: Avg Recovery Score
ml.cart_recovery_score.formula: Avg Recovery Score = average predicted recovery propensity across all scored abandoned carts

ml.carts_scored.label: Abandoned Carts
ml.carts_scored.formula: Abandoned Carts = count of abandoned carts scored by the recovery model this run

ml.category_affinity_customers_scored.label: Customers Scored
ml.category_affinity_customers_scored.formula: Customers Scored = number of customers who received a category recommendation in the latest scoring run

ml.category_affinity_high_confidence_cus.label: High Confidence
ml.category_affinity_high_confidence_cus.formula: High Confidence = count of scored customers whose predicted top category carries a High confidence tier; share = that count ÷ total customers scored

ml.category_affinity_top1_served_custome.label: Single Recommendation
ml.category_affinity_top1_served_custome.formula: Single Recommendation = count of scored customers whose recommendation strategy is a single top-1 category; share = that count ÷ total customers scored

ml.category_affinity_top3_served_custome.label: 3-Category Basket
ml.category_affinity_top3_served_custome.formula: 3-Category Basket = count of scored customers whose recommendation strategy serves three categories; share = that count ÷ total customers scored

ml.category_buyer_breadth_forecast.label: Forecast Buyers (next 4w)
ml.category_buyer_breadth_forecast.formula: Forecast Buyers (next 4w) = sum of the LightGBM-predicted distinct-buyer (reach) score across every category over the next four weeks (approximate reach, not additive across weeks)

ml.category_purchase_propensity_customer.label: Customers Scored
ml.category_purchase_propensity_customer.formula: Customers Scored = number of customers with a category purchase-propensity prediction in the latest run

ml.category_purchase_propensity_high_pro.label: High Propensity Pool
ml.category_purchase_propensity_high_pro.formula: High Propensity Pool = count of customers with high-confidence category purchase predictions; share = that count ÷ total customers scored

ml.category_purchase_propensity_model_au.label: Model AUC
ml.category_purchase_propensity_model_au.formula: Model AUC = area under the ROC curve for the category purchase-propensity model (offline quality metric read from the artifact; 0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect)

ml.category_purchase_propensity_top_oppo.label: Top Opportunity Category
ml.category_purchase_propensity_top_oppo.formula: Top Opportunity Category = the product category with the strongest purchase-propensity signals across all scored customers (read from the precomputed summary artifact)

ml.category_revenue_categories_forecast.label: Categories Forecast
ml.category_revenue_categories_forecast.formula: Categories Forecast = count of distinct categories that have a next-4-week revenue forecast

ml.category_revenue_forecast.label: Forecast Revenue (next 4w)
ml.category_revenue_forecast.formula: Forecast Revenue (next 4w) = sum of the LightGBM-predicted revenue across every category over the next four weeks

ml.category_revenue_forecast_avg_per_cat.label: Avg / Category
ml.category_revenue_forecast_avg_per_cat.formula: Avg per Category = total forecast revenue ÷ number of forecast categories

ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.label: Top-10 Concentration
ml.category_revenue_top10_concentration.formula: Top-10 Concentration = forecast revenue of the 10 highest categories ÷ total forecast revenue

ml.category_revenue_top_category.label: Top Category
ml.category_revenue_top_category.formula: Top Category = the category with the highest forecast revenue, with its share = that category's forecast revenue ÷ total forecast revenue

ml.channel_affinity_avg_confidence.label: Average Confidence
ml.channel_affinity_avg_confidence.formula: Average Confidence = mean predicted channel confidence of the preferred channel across all scored customers

ml.channel_affinity_matched_customers.label: Channel-Matched Customers
ml.channel_affinity_matched_customers.formula: Channel-Matched Customers = count of scored customers whose preferred-channel prediction confidence is above 0.6

ml.channel_affinity_multi_channel_pool.label: Multi-Channel Pool
ml.channel_affinity_multi_channel_pool.formula: Multi-Channel Pool = count of scored customers whose predicted preferred channel is Multi-channel rather than a single channel

ml.channel_affinity_top_channel.label: Top Channel
ml.channel_affinity_top_channel.formula: Top Channel = the most common predicted preferred marketing channel across all scored customers (most frequent preferred channel)

ml.channels_at_saturation.label: Channels at Saturation
ml.channels_at_saturation.formula: Channels at Saturation = count of channels spending above 80% of their model-estimated optimal budget (current spend ÷ optimal spend > 0.8)

ml.churn_probability.label: Average Churn Probability
ml.churn_probability.formula: Average Churn Probability = mean predicted churn probability across all scored customers, shown as a percentage

ml.churn_rate.label: Churn Rate
ml.churn_rate.formula: Churn Rate = customers whose predicted churn probability clears the at-risk threshold ÷ total customers scored

ml.clv_at_risk.label: Total CLV at Risk
ml.clv_at_risk.formula: Total CLV at Risk = sum across customers of (predicted churn probability × predicted customer lifetime value), scaled to the selected horizon

ml.conversion_rate.label: Avg Conversion Rate
ml.conversion_rate.formula: Avg Conversion Rate = mean of the predicted conversion rate across all offer types

ml.customers_scored.label: Customers Scored
ml.customers_scored.formula: Customers Scored = count of distinct customers with a valid churn prediction in this scoring run

ml.declining_trajectory_customers.label: Declining Customers
ml.declining_trajectory_customers.formula: Declining Customers = count of customers predicted to move to a lower value trajectory (predicted class downgrade or big_downgrade)

ml.demand_forecast.label: Forecast Units (next 4w)
ml.demand_forecast.formula: Forecast Units (next 4w) = sum of the LightGBM-predicted unit demand across every product over the next four weeks

ml.demand_forecast_avg_per_product.label: Avg / Product
ml.demand_forecast_avg_per_product.formula: Avg per Product = total forecast units ÷ number of forecast products

ml.demand_forecast_top_product.label: Highest-Demand Product
ml.demand_forecast_top_product.formula: Highest-Demand Product = the product with the highest forecast units, with its share = that product's forecast units ÷ total forecast units

ml.discount_high_responders.label: High Responders
ml.discount_high_responders.formula: High Responders = count of customers in the highest discount-response tier (strongest reactors to promotional pricing)

ml.discount_model_auc.label: Model AUC
ml.discount_model_auc.formula: Model AUC = area under the ROC curve for the discount-response classifier on the holdout set (closer to 1.0 means cleaner separation of responders from non-responders)

ml.discount_responsive_customers.label: Discount-Responsive Customers
ml.discount_responsive_customers.formula: Discount-Responsive Customers = count of customers the model predicts will respond to a discount offer

ml.entry_categories_forecast.label: Entry Categories Forecast
ml.entry_categories_forecast.formula: Entry Categories Forecast = count of distinct entry categories that carry a next-4-week new-customer forecast

ml.expected_retained_value.label: Expected Retained Value (ERV)
ml.expected_retained_value.formula: Expected Retained Value = (1 − average churn probability) × average predicted customer lifetime value × customers scored

ml.first_time_buyers_scored.label: One-Time Buyers Scored
ml.first_time_buyers_scored.formula: One-Time Buyers Scored = count of registered first-time buyers scored by the repeat model this run

ml.forecast_accuracy_mape.label: Forecast Accuracy (MAPE)
ml.forecast_accuracy_mape.formula: Forecast Accuracy (MAPE) = mean absolute percentage error of the demand forecasts on the holdout period (lower is better; read from the training artifact)

ml.forecasted_units_90d.label: Forecasted Units (90d)
ml.forecasted_units_90d.formula: Forecasted Units (90d) = sum of each product's predicted 90-day unit demand across all forecasted products

ml.growth_rate.label: Growth Rate
ml.growth_rate.formula: Growth Rate = flagged growth customers ÷ total scored customers × 100

ml.growth_vs_prior.label: Growth vs Prior Year
ml.growth_vs_prior.formula: Growth vs Prior Year = (forecasted quarter total ÷ actual revenue of the matching weeks 52 weeks earlier − 1) × 100 (blank when fewer than 52 weeks of history exist)

ml.high_confidence_count.label: Reliable Predictions
ml.high_confidence_count.formula: Reliable Predictions = count of customers whose prediction confidence-interval width falls in the narrowest (≤25th percentile) quartile

ml.high_growth_products.label: High-Growth Products (>20%)
ml.high_growth_products.formula: High-Growth Products (>20%) = count of products whose forecasted growth rate exceeds 20%

ml.high_response_pool.label: High-Response Pool
ml.high_response_pool.formula: High-Response Pool = count of customers whose predicted intervention propensity is at or above 50%

ml.high_risk_customers.label: High-Risk Customers
ml.high_risk_customers.formula: High-Risk Customers = count of customers the model places in the High or Critical risk tiers

ml.high_risk_products.label: High-Risk Products
ml.high_risk_products.formula: High-Risk Products = count of products the model flags at Critical or High stock-out risk

ml.high_value_propensity_pool.label: High-Propensity Pool
ml.high_value_propensity_pool.formula: High-Propensity Pool = count of customers scored into the high propensity-to-become-high-value tier

ml.highest_yoy_growth_category.label: Highest YoY Growth Category
ml.highest_yoy_growth_category.formula: Highest YoY Growth Category = the category with the largest year-over-year revenue growth %

ml.immediate_action_required.label: Immediate Action (<7d)
ml.immediate_action_required.formula: Immediate Action (<7d) = count of Critical/High-risk products projected to stock out within the next 7 days

ml.incremental_revenue.label: Total Incremental Revenue
ml.incremental_revenue.formula: Total Incremental Revenue = sum of incremental revenue the Bayesian MMM credits to paid channels (in-sample attribution, baseline/organic excluded)

ml.intervention_propensity.label: Mean Propensity Score
ml.intervention_propensity.formula: Mean Propensity Score = average predicted intervention propensity across all scored customers, shown as a percentage

ml.loyalist_likely_pool.label: Top-Decile Pool (Loyalist Likely)
ml.loyalist_likely_pool.formula: Top-Decile Pool = count of first-time buyers in the highest repeat-propensity segment ('loyalist_likely')

ml.markdown_ready_now.label: Markdown-Ready Now
ml.markdown_ready_now.formula: Markdown-Ready Now = count of products the model flags with markdown urgency = 'Now'

ml.median_confidence_interval_width.label: Typical Prediction Range
ml.median_confidence_interval_width.formula: Typical Prediction Range = median width of the prediction confidence interval across scored customers (in dollars)

ml.message_affinity_avg_confidence.label: Average Confidence
ml.message_affinity_avg_confidence.formula: Average Confidence = mean predicted confidence (softmax probability) of each customer's winning message archetype across all scored customers

ml.message_affinity_customers_segmented.label: Total Customers Segmented
ml.message_affinity_customers_segmented.formula: Total Customers Segmented = number of customers the message-affinity model assigned a preferred message archetype to in the latest run

ml.message_affinity_high_confidence_pool.label: High-Confidence Pool
ml.message_affinity_high_confidence_pool.formula: High-Confidence Pool = count of scored customers whose winning message archetype scored above 50% predicted probability

ml.message_affinity_top_message_type.label: Top Message Type
ml.message_affinity_top_message_type.formula: Top Message Type = the most common predicted preferred message archetype across all scored customers (most frequent winning archetype)

ml.model_auc.label: Model AUC
ml.model_auc.formula: Model AUC = area under the ROC curve on the held-out test set, shown as a percentage (how reliably the model ranks responders above non-responders)

ml.off_peak_revenue_at_risk.label: Off-Peak Revenue at Risk
ml.off_peak_revenue_at_risk.formula: Off-Peak Revenue at Risk = sum of each category's weakest-month (trough) revenue, across all categories

ml.offer_high_priority_pool.label: High-Priority Pool
ml.offer_high_priority_pool.formula: High-Priority Pool = count of customers ranked into the highest-priority tier for offers at the selected horizon

ml.offer_high_priority_share.label: High-Priority Share
ml.offer_high_priority_share.formula: High-Priority Share = high-priority pool ÷ customers scored × 100

ml.offer_lift_customers_scored.label: Customers Scored
ml.offer_lift_customers_scored.formula: Customers Scored = total population the response-score ranker scored for the selected horizon

ml.offer_segment_match_rate.label: Offer-Segment Match Rate
ml.offer_segment_match_rate.formula: Offer-Segment Match Rate = fraction of segment-offer cells flagged as a recommended match in the segment heatmap

ml.offer_top_decile_concentration.label: Top-Decile Concentration
ml.offer_top_decile_concentration.formula: Top-Decile Concentration = top-decile mean response score ÷ overall mean response score (how many times stronger the top 10% of customers' scores are versus the average)

ml.optimal_budget_top_channel.label: Optimal Budget — Top Channel
ml.optimal_budget_top_channel.formula: Optimal Budget — Top Channel = the largest recommended budget across channels in the model's optimal allocation

ml.optimal_discount_range.label: Optimal Discount Range
ml.optimal_discount_range.formula: Optimal Discount Range = the model's metadata range, else the revenue-maximizing discount ± 2.5 percentage points — the band expected to maximise net revenue

ml.organic_revenue.label: Organic Revenue
ml.organic_revenue.formula: Organic Revenue = max(0, total revenue − model-attributed paid revenue) — the baseline revenue not driven by paid media

ml.peak_response_hour.label: Peak Response Hour
ml.peak_response_hour.formula: Peak Response Hour = the most common predicted best-response hour (0–23) across customers (mode of each customer's best hour)

ml.peak_response_rate.label: Peak Response Rate
ml.peak_response_rate.formula: Peak Response Rate = the highest predicted response rate across all discount depths in the response curves

ml.peak_season_category.label: Peak Season Category
ml.peak_season_category.formula: Peak Season Category = the category whose single highest monthly seasonality index is the largest across all categories

ml.peak_week.label: Peak Week
ml.peak_week.formula: Peak Week = the largest single-week forecast revenue across the 13-week horizon, with the week it falls in

ml.personalization_opportunity.label: Personalization Opportunity
ml.personalization_opportunity.formula: Personalization Opportunity = count of customers with a confident, distinct best send time (flagged high-confidence timing)

ml.predicted_new_high_value_customers.label: Predicted New High-Value
ml.predicted_new_high_value_customers.formula: Predicted New High-Value = count of customers the model expects to cross into high-value status

ml.premium_tier_count.label: High-Value Targets
ml.premium_tier_count.formula: High-Value Targets = count of customers the model places in the Premium value tier

ml.price_affinity_avg_wtp_score.label: Avg WTP Score
ml.price_affinity_avg_wtp_score.formula: Avg WTP Score = mean predicted willingness-to-pay score (0–1) across all scored customers

ml.price_affinity_budget_customers.label: Budget Customers
ml.price_affinity_budget_customers.formula: Budget Customers = count of scored customers the model assigns to the Budget price tier

ml.price_affinity_premium_customers.label: Premium Customers
ml.price_affinity_premium_customers.formula: Premium Customers = count of scored customers the model assigns to the Premium price tier

ml.price_affinity_value_seekers.label: Value Seekers
ml.price_affinity_value_seekers.formula: Value Seekers = count of scored customers the model assigns to the Value price tier

ml.price_elasticity_avg_coefficient.label: Avg Elasticity Coefficient
ml.price_elasticity_avg_coefficient.formula: Avg Elasticity Coefficient = mean predicted price-elasticity coefficient across all scored products (values below −1 indicate broadly price-sensitive demand)

ml.price_elasticity_highly_elastic_produ.label: Highly Elastic Products
ml.price_elasticity_highly_elastic_produ.formula: Highly Elastic Products = count of products whose predicted elasticity coefficient is below −1.0 (a small price change moves demand sharply)

ml.price_elasticity_inelastic_products.label: Inelastic Products
ml.price_elasticity_inelastic_products.formula: Inelastic Products = count of products whose predicted elasticity coefficient is between −1.0 and 0 (demand relatively insensitive to price)

ml.price_elasticity_revenue_sensitivity_.label: Revenue Sensitivity Score
ml.price_elasticity_revenue_sensitivity_.formula: Revenue Sensitivity Score = mean of (absolute predicted elasticity × each product's revenue weight) across products — a revenue-weighted measure of how exposed revenue is to price changes

ml.priority_pool.label: High-Propensity Pool
ml.priority_pool.formula: High-Propensity Pool = count of prospects the model places in the top 'priority' activation tier (highest first-purchase propensity)

ml.product_affinity_customers_scored.label: Customers Scored
ml.product_affinity_customers_scored.formula: Customers Scored = number of customers who received a product affinity recommendation in the latest scoring run

ml.product_affinity_high_affinity_pool.label: High Affinity Pool
ml.product_affinity_high_affinity_pool.formula: High Affinity Pool = count of scored customers whose top product recommendation carries a High confidence tier

ml.product_affinity_hit_rate_at_3.label: Hit Rate @3
ml.product_affinity_hit_rate_at_3.formula: Hit Rate @3 = share of customers whose actually-purchased product appeared in the model's top-3 recommendations (offline evaluation metric read from the model artifact)

ml.product_affinity_top_recommended_prod.label: Top Recommended Product
ml.product_affinity_top_recommended_prod.formula: Top Recommended Product = the single product most frequently predicted as a customer's top-1 product across all scored customers (most common top product)

ml.product_revenue_forecast.label: Forecast Revenue (next 4w)
ml.product_revenue_forecast.formula: Forecast Revenue (next 4w) = sum of the LightGBM-predicted revenue across every product over the next four weeks

ml.products_forecast.label: Products Forecast
ml.products_forecast.formula: Products Forecast = count of distinct products that have a next-4-week units forecast

ml.products_forecasted.label: Products Forecasted
ml.products_forecasted.formula: Products Forecasted = count of products the demand model produced a forecast for in this run

ml.projected_incremental_revenue.label: Projected Incremental Revenue
ml.projected_incremental_revenue.formula: Projected Incremental Revenue = the additional revenue the model expects from reallocating spend to its recommended budget mix versus the current allocation

ml.projected_margin_recovery.label: Projected Margin Recovery
ml.projected_margin_recovery.formula: Projected Margin Recovery = sum of the model's projected margin recovery across the markdown-urgency = 'Now' products

ml.quarter_range.label: Quarter Range (80% PI)
ml.quarter_range.formula: Quarter Range (80% PI) = [sum of weekly lower bounds, sum of weekly upper bounds] of the 80% prediction interval across the 13 horizon weeks

ml.quarter_total_forecast.label: Forecast Quarter Total
ml.quarter_total_forecast.formula: Forecast Quarter Total = sum of point-forecast weekly revenue across the next 13 weeks

ml.reallocation_upside.label: Reallocation Upside
ml.reallocation_upside.formula: Reallocation Upside = (optimizer's expected daily response ÷ current daily response − 1) × 100 — extra revenue from shifting the same total budget toward higher-marginal-return channels

ml.repeat_propensity.label: Avg Repeat Propensity
ml.repeat_propensity.formula: Avg Repeat Propensity = average predicted probability that a first-time buyer makes a repeat purchase, across all scored first-time buyers

ml.repeat_propensity_median.label: Median Repeat Propensity
ml.repeat_propensity_median.formula: Median Repeat Propensity = midpoint of the repeat-purchase propensity distribution across scored first-time buyers (half score higher, half lower)

ml.retained_revenue.label: Retained Revenue Forecast
ml.retained_revenue.formula: Retained Revenue Forecast = (1 − average churn probability) × average predicted customer lifetime value × customers scored, shown as the retained share of total predicted revenue

ml.retention_rate.label: Retention Rate
ml.retention_rate.formula: Retention Rate = 1 − churn rate (the share of customers expected to stay over the horizon)

ml.rev_max_discount.label: Revenue-Maximizing Discount
ml.rev_max_discount.formula: Revenue-Maximizing Discount = the discount depth of the row with the highest net revenue (uplift minus discount cost)

ml.revenue_at_stockout_risk.label: Revenue at Stockout Risk
ml.revenue_at_stockout_risk.formula: Revenue at Stockout Risk = sum of the revenue-at-risk across all Critical/High-risk products

ml.revenue_forecast_avg_per_product.label: Avg / Product
ml.revenue_forecast_avg_per_product.formula: Avg per Product = total forecast revenue ÷ number of forecast products

ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.label: Top Product
ml.revenue_forecast_top_product.formula: Top Product = the product with the highest forecast revenue, with its share = that product's forecast revenue ÷ total forecast revenue

ml.revenue_per_top_offer.label: Revenue per Top Offer
ml.revenue_per_top_offer.formula: Revenue per Top Offer = the revenue attributable to the top-converting offer type

ml.revenue_protected_customers.label: Revenue Protected
ml.revenue_protected_customers.formula: Revenue Protected = count of customers predicted NOT to need a discount to convert (would buy at full price, so exclude from discounting)

ml.revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown.label: Revenue Uplift vs No Markdown
ml.revenue_uplift_vs_no_markdown.formula: Revenue Uplift vs No Markdown = model-estimated revenue gain from following the optimal markdown plan versus no markdown action (read from the training artifact)

ml.seasonal_sensitivity.label: Seasonal Sensitivity
ml.seasonal_sensitivity.formula: Seasonal Sensitivity = average of each customer's seasonal responsiveness index — how much customer response swings with the season

ml.stable_high_value_customers.label: Stable High-Value
ml.stable_high_value_customers.formula: Stable High-Value = count of top-tier customers (High, VIP or Whale segment) predicted to hold their value (trajectory class 'hold')

ml.stage_transition_rate.label: Stage Transition Rate
ml.stage_transition_rate.formula: Stage Transition Rate = (customers predicted to improve stage + customers predicted to decline stage) ÷ total customers scored

ml.top_converting_offer.label: Top Converting Offer
ml.top_converting_offer.formula: Top Converting Offer = the offer type with the highest predicted conversion rate across all offer types

ml.top_growth_driver.label: Top Growth Driver
ml.top_growth_driver.formula: Top Growth Driver = the model feature with the highest gain in the grow-probability model (top-ranked feature-importance entry, humanized for display)

ml.top_intervention_type.label: Top Intervention Type
ml.top_intervention_type.formula: Top Intervention Type = the intervention trigger category (e.g. Abandoned Browse / Checkout / Cart) with the highest customer count across scored customers

ml.total_expected_revenue.label: Forecast Revenue
ml.total_expected_revenue.formula: Forecast Revenue = sum of predicted next-cycle order value across all scored customers

ml.total_prospects_scored.label: Total Prospects
ml.total_prospects_scored.formula: Total Prospects = count of prospects (not-yet-first-purchase customers) scored by the activation model this run

ml.total_recommended_budget.label: Total Recommended Budget
ml.total_recommended_budget.formula: Total Recommended Budget = sum of the recommended budgets across all channels under the model's optimal allocation

ml.total_revenue.label: Total Revenue
ml.total_revenue.formula: Total Revenue = model-attributed paid revenue ÷ (1 − organic revenue share) — paid revenue grossed up by the model's organic share so paid + organic sum to the whole

ml.trajectory_customers_scored.label: Customers Scored
ml.trajectory_customers_scored.formula: Customers Scored = total number of customers the value-trajectory model scored this run

ml.trajectory_model_accuracy.label: Model Accuracy
ml.trajectory_model_accuracy.formula: Model Accuracy = top-1 classification accuracy of the trajectory model on the holdout set (how often the predicted class matched the actual outcome)

ml.untapped_low_spend.label: Untapped Low-Spenders
ml.untapped_low_spend.formula: Untapped Low-Spenders = count of growth-flagged customers in the lowest historical AOV (spend) tier

ml.watch_pool.label: Watch Pool
ml.watch_pool.formula: Watch Pool = count of prospects in the middle 'watch' activation tier (moderate first-purchase likelihood)

ml.whale_target_count.label: Top Spenders
ml.whale_target_count.formula: Top Spenders = count of customers flagged as whale targets — predicted high-value orders among the very top spenders

operational.active_alert_rules.label: Active Rules
operational.active_alert_rules.formula: Active Rules = fixed count of enabled alert rules currently monitoring (7)

operational.avg_alert_response_time.label: Avg Response Time
operational.avg_alert_response_time.formula: Avg Response Time = fixed average minutes to acknowledge alerts (15.5)

operational.avg_sync_lag.label: Average Sync Lag
operational.avg_sync_lag.formula: Average Sync Lag = average across schemas of (current time − most recent Fivetran sync time), in minutes

operational.critical_alert_count.label: Critical Alerts
operational.critical_alert_count.formula: Critical Alerts = count of business metrics breaching their critical threshold (today's GA4 revenue < $21,301) + (trailing-7-day Google Ads ROAS < 4.89) + (trailing-7-day Meta Ads CTR < 2.0%)

operational.critical_anomaly_count.label: Critical Anomalies
operational.critical_anomaly_count.formula: Critical Anomalies = count of days in the trailing 7 days where sessions or revenue z-score exceeds 2 standard deviations from the mean

operational.schema_health_pct.label: Schema Health
operational.schema_health_pct.formula: Schema Health % = (fresh + acceptable schemas) ÷ total schemas × 100, where fresh = synced < 60 min ago and acceptable = synced 60–360 min ago

operational.tables_monitored.label: Tables Monitored
operational.tables_monitored.formula: Tables Monitored = count of distinct tables across the GA4, Google Ads, Meta Ads, and Klaviyo source schemas

operational.warning_alert_count.label: Warning Alerts
operational.warning_alert_count.formula: Warning Alerts = count of business metrics in their warning band (today's GA4 revenue in [$21,301, $27,431]) + (trailing-7-day Google Ads ROAS in [4.89, 5.91]) + (trailing-7-day Meta Ads CTR in [2.0%, 2.85%])
